Documentation.short_help: Split dataset into tet volume, then node sets, then side sets as a PartitionedDataSetCollection.
Documentation.long_help: Tetrahedra (if any) are extracted first under vtkDataAssembly IOSS/element_blocks/tetrahedra with contiguous GlobalIds (1..N) for IOSS writers. PartitionedDataSetCollection block order is then all node{i} blocks (node_sets), then all side{i} blocks (side_sets). When Partition Point Array Name is set on the extracted boundary vtkPolyData, vtkThreshold with THRESHOLD_BETWEEN (endpoints inclusive in VTK; All Scalars off: any corner qualifies) keeps cells whose first component lies in (-inf, 0]; vtkGeometryFilter and vtkPolyDataConnectivityFilter plus vtkAppendPolyData merge all such connected components into one side/node pair if non-empty. A second vtkThreshold pass (also any corner, BETWEEN) keeps first component in [1, +inf); vtkPolyDataConnectivityFilter splits that sub-mesh into n connected regions, each its own side/node pair. A cell whose corners all lie strictly in (0, 1) may belong to neither pass. Patch order is the merged non-positive patch first (if present), then the n patches at or above 1 (up to n+1 pairs). If the array is missing or the wrong length, or the name is cleared, or both passes yield no cells, the full surface is split with vtkPolyDataNormals (Feature Angle) and vtkPolyDataConnectivityFilter instead. Sort By Area and Custom Post Reorder apply to the full list of patches. Block metadata sets NAME and vtkIOSSReader ENTITY_ID.

StringVectorProperty.name: PartitionPointArrayName
StringVectorProperty.label: Partition Point Array Name
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: Point-data array on the boundary: first vtkThreshold first component in (-inf, 0] with THRESHOLD_BETWEEN (inclusive endpoints; any corner), merged to one patch; then [1, +inf) (any corner), split by vtkPolyDataConnectivityFilter into n patches (up to n+1 side/node pairs). Default EndpointIndex. Clear to use Feature angle / vtkPolyDataConnectivityFilter on the full surface only.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: FeatureAngle
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Feature angle (deg)
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Used when Partition Point Array Name is empty, or the named array is missing / wrong length, or sign-split threshold yields no patches: vtkPolyDataNormals dihedral threshold for cusp / sharp edge split, then vtkPolyDataConnectivityFilter regions. Larger values merge more smooth neighborhoods.

IntVectorProperty.name: SortByArea
IntVectorProperty.label: Sort By Area
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When enabled (default), reorder all node{i} and side{i} patches by total surface area of each side patch, largest first. When disabled, patch order follows the sign-split construction order (merged <=0 region first, then >=1 connectivity regions), or vtkPolyDataConnectivityFilter region indices on the feature-angle fallback path.

IntVectorProperty.name: CustomPostReorder
IntVectorProperty.label: Custom Post Reorder
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When enabled (default), after the current patch order is fixed (e.g. by Sort By Area), move the third patch to the first position and the first patch to the last position. The second patch follows the new first, then the original fourth through (n-1) in order. No effect if there are fewer than three patches.

IntVectorProperty.name: ComputeBoundaryRadialValue
IntVectorProperty.label: Compute Boundary Radial Value
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: BoundaryRadialValue is always computed as 1 - x^a on side and node set blocks. When this option is enabled, BoundaryRadialValueNormal uses BoundaryRadialValue * sideNormal. When disabled, BoundaryRadialValueNormal uses sideNormal.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: BoundaryRadialNormalFalloffFactor
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Boundary Radial Normal Falloff Factor (a)
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Exponent a in BoundaryRadialValue = 1 - x^a, where x is the raw radial coordinate.

StringVectorProperty.name: BlockNames
StringVectorProperty.label: Block Names
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: Newline-separated names for output partitioned datasets in output order: tetrahedra (if present), all node sets, then all side sets. Empty or missing entries use the default tetrahedra / node{i} / side{i} names.

StringVectorProperty.name: BoundaryVariables
StringVectorProperty.label: Boundary Variables
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: Newline-separated rows aligned with the output block order; values in each row are tab-separated Variable1, Variable2, ... entries. Only side set rows are used. All zero variables disable writing to tetrahedra points for that side. Non-zero values are written to tetrahedra point-data scalars BoundaryVariable1, BoundaryVariable2, ... and also enable BoundaryRadialValueNormal. BoundaryRadialValueNormal does not multiply these variables. If several sides touch the same node, repeated writes are warned and averaged.
